message:
feat(http/h2): expand H/2 connection and stream handling

connection.rs:
  Add 64 lines of connection-level improvements including enhanced
  flow control management and settings frame handling for the HTTP/2
  protocol implementation.

stream.rs:
  Add 18 lines of stream-level refinements for improved multiplexing
  and stream state tracking.

Issue tracker: sync beads state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1ndg","title":"Async I/O Traits Verification Suite (unit tests, adapters)","description":"# Async I/O Traits Verification Suite

## Purpose
Comprehensive verification for async I/O traits (imz, tokio-io equivalent) ensuring correct trait implementations and adapter behavior.

## Test Categories

### 1. Unit Tests
- AsyncRead: poll_read, read_buf
- AsyncWrite: poll_write, poll_flush, poll_shutdown
- AsyncBufRead: poll_fill_buf, consume
- AsyncSeek: poll_complete, start_seek
- Utility: read_to_end, read_to_string, read_exact
- Utility: write_all, copy, duplex

### 2. Adapter Tests
- BufReader: buffered reading
- BufWriter: buffered writing
- BufStream: bidirectional buffering
- Chain: sequential reading
- Take: limited reading
- Split: byte delimiter splitting
- Lines: line iterator
- ReadHalf/WriteHalf: split I/O

### 3. E2E Scenarios
| Scenario | Verifies |
|----------|----------|
| Copy stream | Efficient I/O copy |
| Buffered read chain | Adapter composition |
| Cancel during read_to_end | Partial result safety |
| Duplex echo | Bidirectional I/O |
| Split + join | Reader/writer split |

### 4. Trait Compliance Tests
- Implementors: TcpStream, File, etc.
- Contract verification: flush on drop
- Cancel-safety for all operations

## Logging Requirements
- I/O events logged with buffer sizes
- On failure: dump adapter state
- Byte throughput metrics

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All traits have unit tests
- [ ] All adapters have unit tests
- [ ] E2E scenarios pass
- [ ] Trait compliance verified
- [ ] `cargo test io` passes deterministically

## Verification Commands
```bash
cargo test --package asupersync --lib io
cargo test --test io_e2e

 {"id":"bd-1q6r","title":"[BLOCKER] Fix transport/router.rs compilation errors","description":"The main library does not compile. cargo check --lib fails with 9 errors in src/transport/router.rs.

Key issues:
1. dyn SymbolSink doesn't implement Debug (line 843)
2. Uses self.table instead of self.router.table() (lines 997-999, 1043)
3. Returns RoutingError when function expects DispatchError (lines 1000-1003, 1014, 1058)

Impact: All integration tests blocked, no agents can verify work.

Fix: Manual Debug impl, correct field access, wrap errors in DispatchError::RoutingFailed.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:21:47.274825603Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T08:20:51.948345676Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T08:20:51.948183230Z","close_reason":"Compilation errors fixed - cargo check passes with only warnings","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1rn8","title":"Async Streams Verification Suite (unit tests, E2E, combinators)","description":"# Async Streams Verification Suite

## Purpose
Comprehensive verification for async streams (x3p, tokio-stream equivalent) ensuring combinator correctness and cancel-safety.

## Test Categories

### 1. Unit Tests
- Stream trait: poll_next
- StreamExt: map, filter, filter_map, take, skip
- StreamExt: chain, zip, merge, select
- StreamExt: fold, collect, for_each
- Channel streams: mpsc, broadcast, watch
- Interval stream: tick, tick_with

### 2. E2E Scenarios
| Scenario | Verifies |
|----------|----------|
| Basic iteration | Stream consumption |
| Map + filter chain | Combinator composition |
| Merge streams | Interleaved output |
| Backpressure | Slow consumer handling |
| Cancel mid-stream | Clean termination |
| Infinite stream + take | Bounded consumption |
| Error propagation | Stream error handling |

### 3. Cancel-Safety Tests
- Cancel during next(): no item loss
- Cancel during collect(): partial results
- Cancel merged stream: all sources cancelled

### 4. Memory Tests
- No buffering leaks
- Bounded memory for infinite streams
- Drop semantics for pending items

## Logging Requirements
- Stream events logged with stream IDs
- On failure: dump pending items, combinator state
- Item flow metrics: produced, consumed, dropped

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All combinators have unit tests
- [ ] E2E scenarios pass under lab runtime
- [ ] Cancel-safety verified for all operations
- [ ] Memory bounded for all scenarios
- [ ] `cargo test stream` passes deterministically

## Verification Commands
```bash
cargo test --package asupersync --lib stream
cargo test --test stream_e2e
```","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"ClaudeOpus4.5-Session","created_at":"2026-01-22T19:48:15.585218049Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T05:22:50.755907875Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1rn8","depends_on_id":"asupersync-x3p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:20:45Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-221m","title":"Service Layer Verification Suite (unit tests, E2E, middleware)","description":"# Service Layer Verification Suite

## Purpose
Comprehensive verification for the service layer (lnm, tower equivalent) ensuring composability, cancel-correctness, and middleware stack behavior.

## Test Categories

### 1. Unit Tests
- Service trait: call, poll_ready
- Layer trait: composition
- ServiceBuilder: layer chaining
- Standard middleware: timeout, rate limit, retry, concurrency limit

### 2. E2E Scenarios
| Scenario | Verifies |
|----------|----------|
| Basic service call | Request/response lifecycle |
| Timeout middleware | Budget integration, timeout enforcement |
| Rate limiting | Request throttling, backpressure |
| Retry with backoff | Exponential backoff, max attempts |
| Circuit breaker | Open/half-open/closed states |
| Bulkhead isolation | Resource partitioning |
| Middleware stack | Correct layering order |

### 3. Cancel-Safety Tests
- Cancel mid-request: clean abort
- Cancel with pending retries: no orphan requests
- Cancel with rate limit queue: queue cleanup

### 4. Load Tests
- Concurrent requests
- Backpressure behavior
- Resource exhaustion handling

## Logging Requirements
- Middleware events logged with request IDs
- On failure: dump middleware stack state
- Request/response timings logged

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All middleware have unit tests
- [ ] E2E scenarios pass with real HTTP backend
- [ ] Cancel-safety verified for all middleware
- [ ] Load tests pass without resource leaks
- [ ] `cargo test service` passes deterministically

## Verification Commands
```bash
cargo test --package asupersync --lib service
cargo test --test service_e2e
```","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"PinkMountain","created_at":"2026-01-22T19:47:11.707559049Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T07:17:19.182769279Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T07:17:19.182686816Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-221m","depends_on_id":"asupersync-lnm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:20:45Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-22vb","title":"Codec Framework Verification Suite (unit tests, E2E, framing)","description":"# Codec Framework Verification Suite

## Purpose
Comprehensive verification for the codec framework (4nz, tokio-util codecs equivalent) ensuring correct encoding/decoding and framing.

## Test Categories

### 1. Unit Tests
- Decoder trait: decode, decode_eof
- Encoder trait: encode
- FramedRead: framed decoding
- FramedWrite: framed encoding
- Framed: bidirectional codec
- Common codecs: LinesCodec, BytesCodec, LengthDelimitedCodec

### 2. Codec Implementation Tests
- Lines: newline delimiter
- Bytes: raw passthrough
- LengthDelimited: length-prefixed frames
- JSON: serde_json codec
- Custom codec patterns

### 3. E2E Scenarios
| Scenario | Verifies |
|----------|----------|
| Line-delimited protocol | Text protocol parsing |
| Length-prefixed frames | Binary protocol framing |
| Partial frame handling | Buffer management |
| Large frame handling | Memory efficiency |
| Cancel mid-frame | Clean abort |
| Codec chaining | Nested framing |
| Error propagation | Decode error handling |

### 4. Edge Cases
- Empty frames
- Maximum frame size
- Incomplete frames at EOF
- Malformed input
- Concurrent encode/decode

### 5. Performance Tests
- Throughput for various codecs
- Memory allocation per frame
- Zero-copy where possible

## Logging Requirements
- Codec events logged with frame sizes
- On failure: dump buffer state, codec state
- Frame metrics: encoded, decoded, errors

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All codecs have unit tests
- [ ] E2E scenarios pass
- [ ] Edge cases handled correctly
- [ ] Performance baselines established
- [ ] `cargo test codec` passes deterministically

## Verification Commands
```bash
cargo test --package asupersync --lib codec
cargo test --test codec_e2e

 {"id":"bd-39ik","title":"HTTP Server/Client Verification Suite (unit tests, E2E, protocol)","description":"# HTTP Server/Client Verification Suite

## Purpose
Comprehensive verification for the HTTP layer (if7, hyper equivalent) ensuring protocol compliance, cancel-correctness, and performance.

## Test Categories

### 1. Unit Tests
- Request parsing: methods, headers, body
- Response construction: status, headers, body
- HTTP/1.1: keep-alive, chunked encoding, pipelining
- HTTP/2: streams, flow control, HPACK
- Connection management: pooling, reuse

### 2. E2E Scenarios
| Scenario | Verifies |
|----------|----------|
| GET request/response | Basic HTTP lifecycle |
| POST with body | Request body handling |
| Streaming response | Chunked transfer |
| Keep-alive reuse | Connection pooling |
| HTTP/2 multiplexing | Stream concurrency |
| Cancel mid-request | Client abort handling |
| Server shutdown | Graceful drain |
| Large body transfer | Memory efficiency |

### 3. Protocol Compliance Tests
- RFC 9110 (HTTP Semantics)
- RFC 9112 (HTTP/1.1)
- RFC 9113 (HTTP/2)
- Header validation
- Content-Length vs Transfer-Encoding

### 4. Performance Tests
- Requests per second baseline
- Concurrent connection handling
- Memory usage under load
- Latency distribution (p50, p95, p99)

### 5. Security Tests
- Request smuggling prevention
- Header injection prevention
- Timeout enforcement
- Resource exhaustion protection

## Logging Requirements
- HTTP events logged with request IDs
- On failure: dump connection state, pending requests
- Timing breakdown: parsing, processing, serialization

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Protocol compliance tests pass
- [ ] E2E scenarios cover HTTP/1.1 and HTTP/2
- [ ] Cancel-safety verified for client and server
- [ ] Performance baselines established
- [ ] `cargo test http` passes deterministically

## Verification Commands
```bash
cargo test --package asupersync --lib http
cargo test --test http_e2e
cargo bench --bench http_perf
```","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"PinkMountain","created_at":"2026-01-22T19:47:38.086653259Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T05:40:35.738379666Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T05:40:35.738315567Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-39ik","depends_on_id":"asupersync-if7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:20:45Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3cg4","title":"[Bug] Cx::race does not cancel loser tasks - violates losers-are-drained invariant","description":"## Summary

The Cx::race method in src/cx/cx.rs:1240-1254 does NOT cancel loser tasks.

## Evidence
Test tests/repro_race_leak.rs demonstrates is_cancelled=false for loser after Cx::race.

## Root Cause
Cx::race just drops losers without calling abort() or awaiting drain.

## Fix
Need to properly cancel and drain losers like Scope::race does.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"AmberLantern","created_at":"2026-01-28T22:19:39.654367622Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T22:33:11.155505654Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T22:33:11.155329627Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-3h3y","title":"Fix plan_rewrite_equivalence tests - runtime not quiescent","description":"## Problem
Three tests in phase0_verification.rs failing with 'runtime quiescent after reschedule: expected true, got false'

## Investigation Summary (by GoldGate)

### Tests Failing
- plan_rewrite_equivalence_lab_runtime
- plan_rewrite_equivalence_lab_runtime_nonbinary  
- plan_rewrite_equivalence_lab_runtime_shared_non_leaf

### Root Cause Analysis
1. Pre-existing bug: Present since at least commit 4a1b126
2. Flow: run_until_quiescent() -> not quiescent -> reschedule runnable tasks -> run_until_quiescent() again -> still not quiescent
3. Quiescence check: is_quiescent() requires live_task_count()==0, pending_obligation_count()==0, and no I/O activity
4. Default max_steps: LabConfig has max_steps=100,000 which may be causing early termination

### Likely Causes
- Tasks stuck in non-terminal states (CancelRequested, Cancelling, Finalizing)
- Scheduler not making progress on scheduled tasks
- Task state machine bug where tasks don't transition to Completed

### Next Steps
1. Add debug logging to identify stuck tasks and their states
2. Check if max_steps is being hit prematurely
3. Investigate task state transitions in PlanDag execution","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-28T19:25:49.812517066Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T19:46:51.270890204Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T19:46:51.270822869Z","close_reason":"Schedule plan test tasks with budget priority to prevent starvation; plan_rewrite_equivalence tests pass","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-4rpn","title":"[Harmonize] Fix priority alignments","description":"# [Harmonize] Fix priority alignments

## Purpose
Ensure priority follows dependency order: parent EPICs have priority ≤ child priorities.

## Issues Fixed
- xrc (Phase 1) was P2, now P1 (blocks ds8 which is P2)
- 90l9 (P0 duplicate) closed/merged into ds8

## Rule
Priority must respect dependencies:
- If A blocks B, then priority(A) ≤ priority(B)
- P0 = Critical, P1 = High, P2 = Medium, P3 = Low, P4 = Backlog

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No priority inversions in dependency graph
- [ ] `bv --robot-priority` shows no misalignments
- [ ] All xrc.* sub-tasks have P1 priority
- [ ] Parent EPICs never have higher priority number than children

## Verification
```bash
bv --robot-priority | jq '.recommendations'  # Should be empty or acceptable
```","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ClaudeOpus45","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:28:29.875907033Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T03:14:34.794684288Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T02:35:47.483747422Z","close_reason":"Fixed priority inversions:
- 14o: P0→P1 (match P1 dependencies)
- w9rc: P0→P1 (blocked by 14o)
- 4qw: P2→P1 (match P1 children)
- 4ul: P1→P2 (blocked by ds8)
- 6ll,9mq: P1→P3 (blocked by P3 items)
- y1p,0vx,ucq,zfn: P1→P2 (cascading fixes)
- tpru: P2→P3, brm: P2→P3, ou7o: P2→P3, 3nm: P2→P3
- z8n,5jm,m76: P1→P2 (blocked by 4ul)
No cycles detected. Core xrc.* tasks are P1, stretch goals (xrc.5,xrc.6) appropriately P3/P4.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-4rpn","depends_on_id":"bd-zs64","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:20:45Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":11,"issue_id":"bd-4rpn","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Ran a priority inversion scan against .beads/issues.jsonl: 42 inversions where a blocker has lower priority (numerically higher). Notables: asupersync-xrc P1 blocks bd-hzrb P0 (also asupersync-n5o P1 blocks bd-hzrb P0), asupersync-akx.2.2 P1 blocks asupersync-akx.2 P0, asupersync-jdg P1 blocks asupersync-akx.7 P0, asupersync-0a0 P2 blocks multiple P1 tasks (4v1/573/9r7/h10/uls/xtx), asupersync-r2n P2 blocks 4v1/9r7/fxd P1. Full list available on request.","created_at":"2026-01-23T03:14:34Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-5u7k","title":"Fix UnixDatagram Sync impl and refactor registration","description":"# Fix UnixDatagram Sync impl and refactor registration

## Investigation Summary

The `UnixDatagram` uses `Mutex<Option<IoRegistration>>` for interior mutability, which differs from tokio's approach where `PollEvented` handles registration internally without user-visible Mutex.

### Current Pattern (UnixDatagram, TcpListener, tcp/split.rs)
- `registration: Mutex<Option<IoRegistration>>` visible in struct
- Lock acquired on each async I/O operation

### Tokio Pattern (for reference)
- `io: PollEvented<mio::net::UnixDatagram>` - No Mutex needed
- Registration handled internally with separate read/write wakers

## Issue Analysis

1. **Not a safety bug**: Current code is memory-safe - std::os::unix::net::UnixDatagram is Send + Sync, and Mutex<T> is Send + Sync if T: Send.

2. **Performance concern**: Each async operation acquires the Mutex lock, causing contention in concurrent use.

3. **Pattern inconsistency**: This pattern is used across multiple socket types (UnixDatagram, TcpListener, tcp/split), suggesting need for a shared PollEvented or AsyncIo abstraction.

## Recommended Solution

Create a PollEvented<S: Source> wrapper that:
- Handles registration internally
- Maintains separate read/write wakers for concurrent operations
- Can be shared via Arc without external Mutex

## Relationship to Phase 2

This refactoring aligns with asupersync-90l9 (Phase 2: Real Async I/O Reactor). Recommend implementing PollEvented as part of Phase 2, then refactoring all socket types.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:54:44.411557169Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T07:46:43.003274483Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T07:46:43.003223467Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-hszn","title":"gRPC Framework Verification Suite (unit tests, E2E, streaming)","description":"# gRPC Framework Verification Suite

## Purpose
Comprehensive verification for the gRPC framework (3or, tonic equivalent) ensuring protocol compliance, streaming correctness, and cancel-safety.

## Test Categories

### 1. Unit Tests
- Service definitions: codegen output
- Request/Response types: encoding, decoding
- Metadata: headers, trailers
- Status codes: mapping, propagation
- Interceptors: client and server

### 2. Streaming Pattern Tests
- Unary: single request, single response
- Server streaming: single request, stream response
- Client streaming: stream request, single response
- Bidirectional: stream request, stream response

### 3. E2E Scenarios
| Scenario | Verifies |
|----------|----------|
| Unary RPC | Basic request/response |
| Server streaming | Large result set |
| Client streaming | Upload pattern |
| Bidirectional chat | Real-time communication |
| Deadline propagation | Timeout inheritance |
| Cancel mid-stream | Clean termination |
| Metadata round-trip | Header/trailer handling |
| Reflection service | Service discovery |

### 4. Protocol Compliance Tests
- gRPC over HTTP/2
- Message framing
- Compression: gzip, deflate, none
- Content-Type validation
- Status code mapping

### 5. Load Tests
- Concurrent RPCs
- Long-running streams
- Connection multiplexing
- Backpressure handling

### 6. Security Tests
- TLS integration
- mTLS client auth
- Token validation

## Logging Requirements
- RPC events logged with method names
- On failure: dump message state, stream state
- Timing: request/response latency per RPC

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All streaming patterns have tests
- [ ] Protocol compliance verified
- [ ] Cancel-safety for all patterns
- [ ] Load tests pass without leaks
- [ ] `cargo test grpc` passes deterministically

## Verification Commands
```bash
cargo test --package asupersync --lib grpc
cargo test --test grpc_e2e

 {"id":"bd-js88","title":"Async Filesystem Verification Suite (unit tests, E2E, fault injection)","description":"# Async Filesystem Verification Suite

## Purpose
Comprehensive verification for async filesystem operations (aqy, tokio-fs equivalent) ensuring cancel-safety and data integrity.

## Test Categories

### 1. Unit Tests
- File: open, create, read, write, seek
- File: sync_all, sync_data, set_len
- Directory: create_dir, create_dir_all, remove_dir
- Directory: read_dir, canonicalize
- Metadata: metadata, symlink_metadata
- Permissions: set_permissions
- Links: hard_link, symlink, read_link
- Remove: remove_file, remove_dir_all
- Rename: rename
- Copy: copy

### 2. E2E Scenarios
| Scenario | Verifies |
|----------|----------|
| Read entire file | Basic file I/O |
| Write + sync | Durability semantics |
| Append to file | Concurrent append safety |
| Cancel mid-write | Two-phase write safety |
| Directory traversal | Recursive listing |
| Large file copy | Streaming copy |
| Atomic rename | POSIX rename semantics |
| Temp file workflow | Create, write, rename |

### 3. Fault Injection Tests
- ENOENT: File not found
- EACCES: Permission denied
- ENOSPC: No space left
- EINTR: Interrupted
- Partial write (disk full mid-write)
- Concurrent file access

### 4. Two-Phase Tests
- Reserve write buffer
- Commit on success
- Abort on cancel/error
- No partial writes visible

## Logging Requirements
- File events logged with file paths and handles
- On failure: dump I/O operation state
- Byte counts: read, written, synced

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All file operations have unit tests
- [ ] E2E scenarios pass under lab runtime
- [ ] Fault injection tests verify error handling
- [ ] Two-phase semantics enforced
- [ ] `cargo test fs` passes deterministically

## Verification Commands
```bash
cargo test --package asupersync --lib fs
cargo test --test fs_e2e
